auditData.title: Mount Sinai Hospital

page_excerpts.text:
Education and Training Programs
MSI offers customized training programs and formal degree programs, including the Master of Public Health (MPH) and the Master of Science in Health Care Delivery Leadership, along with continuing medical education (CME) classes.

Customized Training Programs
MSI has the unique ability to offer customized training programs for all members of the health care enterprise. Through our extensive health care delivery system, which includes seven hospital campuses, over 300 ambulatory sites, and our extensive graduate medical education and management platforms, we have the ability and resources to train individuals and large cohorts alike. Physicians, nurses, management, and non-clinical staff members all have the opportunity to participate in these programs, which may include, but are not limited to, leadership development, fellowships, and observerships.

A one and one half-day intensive course for 30 Spanish hospital pharmacy executives, which included clinical and non-clinical topics and facility tours
A two-week intensive global nursing... and training program for a multi-disciplinary 'heart team' from a health system in Colombia
A two-day intensive course for 30 Italian hospital leaders
Clinical observerships for a physician, nurse, and pharmacist from health system in Singapore

auditData.title: Montefiore / Albert Einstein

page_excerpts.text:
Graduate Medical Education
Montefiore Einstein is one of the nation's preeminent centers for graduate medical education, drawing top talent globally and producing industry leaders committed to advancing human health.
Residency & Fellowship Programs

Montefiore Einstein Anesthesiology offers observership opportunities for medical students and external residents, providing broad exposure and immersive observation across a range of treatments and techniques.
To apply, please reach out to a supervising physician in your subspecialty of interest. Attending anesthesiologists in each of our subspecialities can be found on our Team page.
After receiving confirmation from the faculty member, email your CV and requested observership dates to Debbie Lopez, Anesthesiology Graduate Medical Education Administrator...
Please note there will be a formal onboarding process with Montefiore Einstein's House Staff Office for all observerships.
We welcome applications from both internal and external residents.

Applicant Eligibility
Visiting students are eligible for electives at CUIMC if they meet the following criteria:
Currently enrolled in their final year of medical school at one of the following:
Domestic: LCME-accredited school in the US/Canada, Osteopathic school
International: International Exchange Affiliation (must be pre-approved by home school)
Completed at least 4-6 core clerkships in Internal Medicine, Surgery, and 2 other specialties (including the elective specialty in which they are applying, where available).

page_excerpts.text: Fourth-year medical students who are interested in high-acuity urban emergency care are encouraged to apply for a visiting elective in Emergency Medicine (EM) at the Emergency Medicine Residency Program at Montefiore Einstein and NYC Health + Hospitals | Jacobi, which is affiliated with Albert Einstein College of Medicine.

This four-week rotation offers an immersive clinical experience across our three main campuses:
NYC Health + Hospitals | Jacobi, a Level I Adult and Level II Pediatric Trauma Center
Montefiore Einstein, Moses Campus, a high-volume tertiary referral center
Jack D. Weiler Hospital, a tertiary care facility on the Einstein Campus, with high-acuity mixed patient care

All visiting student applications must be submitted through the Association of American Medical Colleges (AAMC) Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) platform. Elective opportunities are available for students from Liaison Committee on Medical Education (LCME)-accredited U.S. medical schools. The elective usually fills quickly, so early application is recommended.

Key requirements include:
Fourth-year status at an LCME-accredited medical school
Completion of core clerkships (including Medicine, Surgery and Pediatrics)
Immunization and health clearance
Proof of malpractice insurance
Upload of supporting documents via VSLO

Elective title: Emergency Medicine, Albert Einstein College of Medicine
Site: NYC Health + Hospitals | Jacobi and Montefiore Einstein
Schedule: Four weeks (specific dates assigned based on availability)

title: Elmhurst Hospital Center
shortDescription: NYC H+H public hospital — one of the most diverse hospitals in the world (168 languages spoken). Very IMG-friendly with excellent clinical volume. Mount Sinai affiliated.
fullDescription:
The MOSAIC Visiting Scholars Program (VSP) offers four-week at NYC Health + Hospitals for medical students and trainees who are interested in serving H+H's diverse patient populations. The goal is to have the NYC Health + Hospitals' physician workforce that better reflects our patient population by creating experiential, clinical, and mentorship opportunities across NYC Health + Hospitals.
MOSAIC VSP will run two cohorts per year. Each VSP participant will be matched to a NYC H+H participating site and will conduct clinical shadowing, didactic sessions, practical learnings, simulation and experiential training. The goal is to allow participants to develop and refine their professional development skills, gain clinical exposure and strengthen their commitment to social justice and serving vulnerable populations. The rotations will take place between August – November, 2026 and January – April, 2027, depending on the availability of the placement.

Participants accepted into MOSAIC VSP will be provided a $2,000 stipend for the rotation and an additional $2,000 housing stipend for participants that are not based in the New York City metro area. Students from the NYC metro area are expected to cover their own housing costs. Students from underrepresented backgrounds are encouraged to apply.
eligibilitySummary: MD/MBBS, USMLE Step 1, health clearance, immunizations | Visa: B1/B2

title: Stanford Health Care
shortDescription: Visiting Observer Program. Max 30 days in 12 months. Requires faculty host. Fees vary by department ($1,500 for practicing physicians in some departments). Processing ~8 weeks. No visa sponsorship.
fullDescription:
SHC Tri-Valley Visiting Observer Program
Stanford Tri-Valley Hospital is pleased to offer a visiting observer opportunity through our Visiting Observer Program Team. Observers must be 18 years or older and have a host to apply for this program.
Steps Involved in the Visiting Observer (VO) Program:
Potential VOs must reach out to a member of the Tri-Valley workforce to be their Host. The Visiting Observer Program Team does not match potential VOs and Hosts. It is the VO's responsibility to find a Host and obtain their agreement.
Once a Host has agreed, they reach out to VisitingObserver@stanfordhealthcare.org with subject "SHC Tri-Valley VO".
The Visiting Observer Program Team review intake forms and provide the Host with a packet... The Host must sign an attestation... The VO must: review Code of Conduct + HIPAA, sign VO Attestation, be medically cleared by Occupational Health.
Timeline and Expectations:
Hosts must submit intake form a minimum of 3 weeks before first proposed day of observation.
VOs are limited to 30 days of observation within a twelve-month period.
If the potential VO is a member of the Stanford workforce, there is an expedited medical clearance process.
FAQ — Who can be a Visiting Observer?
VOs can be any individual who is at least 18 years of age who is interested in watching patient care at Stanford Health Care Tri-Valley. Note that VOs may not participate in [continues below cut...]

page_excerpts.text:
The Visiting Students Program at Harvard Medical School (HMS) offers eligible medical students the opportunity to complete a visiting clerkship at one of the Harvard-affiliated hospitals.
The HMS Visiting Students Program is one of the largest visiting student programs in the country. The program is for medical students in their final year of medical school who wish to participate in clinical clerkships offered at HMS-affiliated teaching hospitals.

Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center · Brigham and Women's Hospital · Cambridge Health Alliance · Boston Children's Hospital · Dana-Farber Cancer Institute · Massachusetts Eye and Ear · Massachusetts General Hospital · McLean Hospital · Mount Auburn Hospital · Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
HMS does not offer research opportunities and/or observerships for visiting students.

Must be currently enrolled medical student in the final year of medical school (within 12 months of graduation).
Must be in good academic standing at an approved medical school.
Must have completed satisfactorily the core clinical clerkship requirements comparable to HMS core (minimally, surgery, medicine, pediatrics & OB/GYN). Applicants applying for psychiatry, neurology and/or radiology will be required to have satisfactorily completed core clinical clerkships in these specialties.
Must be in compliance with HMS immunization requirements – HMS strictly adheres to these immunization guidelines.
Must be fluent in the English language including: conversation, writing, and reading.
Anyone holding an MD degree, or its equivalent, is not eligible.
Our clerkships are offered in four-week blocks and visiting students must adhere to HMS rotation dates as posted in the calendar.

Harvard Medical School is now accepting applications from international students enrolled in one of the medical schools listed below for rotations from October to March only.
If you are an international student enrolled at a school not listed below, you are unfortunately not eligible for elective study through Harvard Medical School (HMS), there are no exceptions. HMS is not seeking any new affiliation agreements at this time.

auditData.title: Northwestern Memorial Hospital (Feinberg Visiting Students)  [IL · Chicago] (MISLABEL — VSLO ELECTIVE, US-only with Global Partner exception)

page_excerpts.text:
Apply now in VSLO!
We welcome visiting medical students from other institutions to apply to electives at Northwestern University Feinberg School of Medicine, located in the Streeterville neighborhood of Chicago along beautiful Lake Michigan. Our location gives students access to our top-rated clinical affiliates — including Northwestern Memorial Hospital, the Ann and Robert H. Lurie Children's Hospital of Chicago and the Shirley Ryan AbilityLab — as well as to the city of Chicago's vibrant cultural landscape.

Each year, the Visiting Student Program at Feinberg School of Medicine receives thousands of applications for a limited number of elective rotation spaces. Eligible participants must be full-time, fourth-year medical students in good standing at LCME- or AOA-accredited U.S. medical schools. Third-year students who have completed their core clerkships in medicine, surgery, pediatrics and obstetrics/gynecology are also eligible to apply.

Do not contact course directors, coordinators or faculty for any reason during the application process. Contacting any of these parties may result in your ineligibility for current and future rotations.

Elective spaces are extremely limited, so our electives are available only to students currently enrolled at an LCME-accredited medical school or AOA-accredited Osteopathic medical school located in the United States or Puerto Rico. We cannot accept students from schools located outside the contiguous 48 states, Alaska, Hawaii and Puerto Rico, with the exception of Global Partner institutions.

Rush Medical College welcomes applications for visiting medical students from LCME-accredited or COCA-accredited medical schools only, who want to pursue clinical elective rotations at Rush University Medical Center. Applicants must be currently enrolled in medical school, be in good academic standing and have completed all required M3 core rotations prior to the elective time period.
Rush Medical College uses the Association of American Medical Colleges' Visiting Student Application Service (VSAS) to receive and process visiting student applications. Electives are available if space permits. Applications for each rotation are due eight weeks before the rotation begins.

page_excerpts.text: Stritch uses the Visiting Student Application Service (VSLO), to receive visiting student applications. Our VSLO Catalog will be available for viewing late January 2026 for AY2026-2027. The earliest students will be accepted is February 2nd, 2026 for the May 2026 period. There is a rolling acceptance for each subsequent period.

May 4-29, 2026 · June 1-26, 2026 · June 29-July 24, 2026 · August 3-28, 2026 · August 31-September 25, 2026 · September 28-October 23, 2026 · October 26-November 20, 2026 · November 23-December 18, 2026 · January 4-29, 2027 · February 1-26, 2027 · March 1-25, 2027 · April 5-30, 2027

Loyola University Chicago Stritch School of Medicine requires students to show proof of surveillance for tuberculosis infection within 12 months of their scheduled clinical rotation and proof of immunization against Measles, Mumps, Rubella, Varicella, Hepatitis B, and Tetanus, Diphtheria & Pertussis (TDAP vaccine). You also must include lab report PDF's with numeric titer results to prove your Measles, Mumps, Rubella, Varicella, and Hepatitis B immunity. Proof of annual influenza immunization required for rotations between November 1 and April 30.

We only accept applications through an electronic application service from the AAMC called Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO)... SSOM is a Host institution, meaning that we will host students for visiting elective clerkships from schools that are Home school members of VSLO.

If you decide to accept our elective offer, we require payment of a US $1500 acceptance fee (per elective) within 14 days of your elective offer (or your registration will be administratively canceled).

An elective rotation at Loyola University Chicago Stritch School of Medicine is designed to be a highly-valued educational experience. It should not be viewed as an 'audition' for a residency position nor should there be any expectation that this experience will lead to an interview.

You must be actively enrolled in an MD or MBBS program at a medical school that is a member of VSLO and be in your final year of medical school at the time of the elective rotation you are seeking. Your medical school must certify that they will accept the elective clerkship(s) for credit. We cannot accept students who have already graduated and received an MD or MBBS degree.

You must have successfully completed the following core clinical clerkships or will complete them prior to the elective rotation: internal medicine, surgery, pediatrics, obstetrics/gynecology, and psychiatry.
A passing score on USMLE Step 1 is required.
If you are not a native English speaker, you must submit a TOEFL or IELTS score as proof of your English proficiency.

Professional liability/medical malpractice insurance at limits of US$1 million (per incident) and US$3 million (aggregate) will be provided, during the period of enrollment, for any accepted international visiting student by Stritch who isn't already covered.
Visiting students may enroll for a maximum of 12 elective weeks. The electives available to international students are all four weeks in length at Loyola.

If you are not a US citizen or permanent resident, you must obtain the appropriate visa and provide us with such documentation. Due to visa requirements, you may not work in any capacity or be provided with any remuneration for activities while at Loyola.

title: Cleveland Clinic
shortDescription: IMPORTANT: NOT for IMGs seeking US residency — applications from residency-seeking IMGs will NOT be considered. For international physicians practicing abroad, residents, fellows, and medical students only. 1 month. No direct patient care.
fullDescription:
Overview
The Observership program is an informal shadowing experience that enables participants to watch procedures and surgeries; attend patient rounds and teaching conferences; and have full access to Cleveland Clinic's medical library system. Federal regulations prevent observers from having patient care responsibilities or involvement in any form of volunteer research.

Who is eligible?
The International Physician Observership Program is open to:
Fully trained international physicians currently practicing abroad.
International residents and fellows enrolled in a residency/fellowship program at a foreign institution and who are expected to graduate from that same institution.
International medical students enrolled at an international medical school and who are expected to graduate from that same school after the prospective visit would take place. Medical students who are in the last year of medical school at the time of their application are not eligible. Eligible students who are in their final year of medical school may consider applying for the Elective Program.
eligibilitySummary: MD/MBBS, must be practicing abroad or in training internationally. NOT for IMGs in US seeking residency. | Visa: B1/B2

auditData.title: Cleveland Clinic — International Physician Observership Program

page_excerpts.text:
Overview
The Observership program is an informal shadowing experience that enables participants to watch procedures and surgeries; attend patient rounds and teaching conferences; and have full access to Cleveland Clinic's medical library system. Federal regulations prevent observers from having patient care responsibilities or involvement in any form of volunteer research.

Who is eligible?
The International Physician Observership Program is open to:
Fully trained international physicians currently practicing abroad.
International residents and fellows enrolled in a residency/fellowship program at a foreign institution and who are expected to graduate from that same institution.
International medical students enrolled at an international medical school and who are expected to graduate from that same school after the prospective visit would take place. Medical students who are in the last year of medical school at the time of their application are not eligible. Eligible students who are in their final year of medical school may consider applying for the Elective Program.

The International Physician Observership Program is not designed for international medical graduates (IMG) residing in the United States or abroad, who intend to apply for residency or fellowship in the U.S.

At this time, Cleveland Clinic Center for International Medical Education is not accepting applicants who are citizens of the following countries: Belarus, Cuba, Iran, Libya, North Korea, Russia, and Venezuela.

auditData.title: Johns Hopkins Hospital (Observerships)  [MD · Baltimore]

page_excerpts.text: Careers in health care include patient care, visitor relations, business functions and more! If you are considering a career in health care, becoming an observer of these roles may be of interest to you. An observership is an educational experience that allows you to shadow a member of the medical staff or workforce (a sponsor) and learn more about the field.

An individual who desires an in-person educational experience that is best acquired by closely observing or 'shadowing' a health care worker.
Observers must be 16 or older.
Observers under the age of 18 must provide a signed parental consent form.
May include high school and college students, and those who are not currently enrolled in school but are interested in the health care industry.

An observership at Johns Hopkins Medicine:
Shall not exceed 100 hours over the course of 12 months.
Does not constitute medical training or award academic credit.
May be available at any of our participating entities:
The Johns Hopkins Hospital
Johns Hopkins Bayview Medical Center
Howard County General Hospital
Sibley Memorial Hospital
Suburban Hospital
Johns Hopkins All Children's Hospital
Johns Hopkins Community Physicians

The observer must identify and obtain agreement from a sponsor prior to applying for an observership. Once identified, the sponsor directs the observer to initiate a Request for an Observership with Johns Hopkins.

Observers are required to:
Create a user account in the online registration system.
Indicate the sponsor's name and email on the application.
Confirm dates and location of the observership.
Complete and submit for approval the required documentation in the Johns Hopkins Observer Application.
This includes:
Parental consent, if under 18
Required vaccination records
Confidentiality Pledge for Visitors
Bloodborne pathogen acknowledgement

Observers are not issued an employee ID badge, JHED ID, or keys.
Observers who have had a known exposure to a contagious agent, who have active colds or infections, or who do not feel well are not permitted in the health care facility.
The observer shall not wear a lab coat or scrubs unless required for the observation area, and shall return to normal dress as soon as possible.
The sponsor/designee ensures that the student observer DOES NOT have physical contact with patients and does not have access to the electronic medical record system.
If the observership involves clinical care, patients have the right to refuse having an observer present during examinations and discussions. If a patient refuses to permit an observer to be present during examinations and discussions, the request must be honored.

The Volunteer Services Office does not match individuals with observership opportunities. Prospective observers must independently research health care providers at Johns Hopkins hospitals or health care centers and secure observership dates with a sponsor. To find a physician or dentist, go to hopkinsmedicine.org and click on 'Doctors.'
While many Johns Hopkins providers enjoy teaching interested individuals about medicine, there is no guarantee or obligation for physicians to take on observers. Please be polite, and give physicians plenty of time to set up the observership, as well as the right to refuse.

auditData.title: Mayo Clinic  [MN · Rochester] (MISLABEL — VSLO ELECTIVE/Clerkship, IMG via VSLO Home schools only, NOT an observership)

page_excerpts.text: Mayo Clinic's Visiting Medical Student Clerkship Program offers students a broad array of elective clinical rotations that foster the growth of medical knowledge through direct patient care experiences. The program provides elective clinical rotations for over 600 participating medical students each year. Visiting students receive diverse hands-on experiences in our program.

3 locations · #5 Top Hospitals and Health Systems for Fairness · 107 electives to choose from"
"Mayo Clinic has hosted visiting medical student clerkships since 1915.
Phoenix/Scottsdale, AZ · Jacksonville, FL · Rochester, MN

Each year, the Visiting Medical Student Clerkship Program at Mayo Clinic receives more applications than it has educational opportunities. Selection is determined by the clinical departments and based on medical school transcript grades, successful completion of USMLE/COMLEX Step 1, space availability, and competitiveness for a Mayo Clinic residency.
page_excerpts.section_heading: Eligibility (verbatim from application-process sub-page, verified — CRITICAL)

page_excerpts.text:
Candidates for the Visiting Medical Student Clerkship Program at Mayo Clinic must:
Have passed the United States Medical Licensing Examination (USMLE) Step 1, Comprehensive Osteopathic Medical Licensing Examination (COMLEX) Level 1, or National Board Dental Examination (NBDE); international applicants must have passed USMLE Step 1
Be in the final year of medical school at the time of the clerkship. Dental students must be in the junior or senior year of dental school or enrolled in an advanced dental education or prosthodontics certificate program in the U.S. or Canada
Be in good academic standing at their medical school
Have received the required immunizations by the start of their clerkship rotation
page_excerpts.section_heading: Eligibility (verbatim from application-process sub-page, verified — CRITICAL)

page_excerpts.text:
The Mayo Clinic Visiting Medical Student Clerkship Program uses the Association of American Medical Colleges (AAMC), Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) portal for accepting and processing clerkship applications.
The Visiting Medical Student Clerkship program will not be accepting any applications outside of the VSLO portal. For international students whose medical school is not currently an AAMC participating institution, please see the AAMC website for information on how to initiate the process of having your institution enroll in the application service.

Dec. 1 (noon ET) – Dec. 31 (8 p.m. ET): rotations beginning April-June
March 1 – March 31: rotations beginning July-Sept.
June 1 – June 30: rotations beginning Oct.-Dec.
Sept. 1 – Sept. 30: rotations beginning Jan.-March

Clerkships are always four weeks in length, beginning on a Monday and concluding four weeks later on a Friday in full-week increments (with the exception of holidays).
You may apply to only one Mayo Clinic location. Candidates may submit up to four total applications.
students accepted to our clerkship program are limited to one, one-month experience. Additional rotations or extending rotations beyond the allotted time is not permitted.

Most programs require one letter of recommendation for domestic candidates and two letters of recommendation for global candidates."
Specialty-specific: 2 LORs required for Cardiovascular Diseases (AZ), General/Vascular/Colorectal Surgery (FL), Neurology (AZ), Oncology Surgery (FL), PM&R (MN), Women's Health (AZ); 4 LORs for Radiation Oncology (MN); 0 LORs for Radiation Oncology (FL).
Acceptance and participation in Mayo Clinic's Visiting Medical Student Clerkship Program does not guarantee a letter of recommendation or residency interview.

Rochester, Minnesota: clerkship@mayo.edu
Jacksonville, Florida: flacomvisit@mayo.edu
Phoenix/Scottsdale, Arizona: arzclerkship@mayo.edu
Mayo Clinic Health System: MCHSclerkship@mayo.edu

auditData.title: University of Minnesota Medical Center  [MN · Minneapolis] (MISLABEL — VSLO US M4 ELECTIVE, IMG via formal agreement only)

page_excerpts.text:
Visiting medical students from domestic LCME-accredited medical schools and COCA-accredited medical schools ONLY may apply for selected elective rotations at the University of Minnesota Medical School.
International medical students are not accepted unless a formal agreement exists between their medical school and the University of Minnesota. For a list of medical schools with whom the University of Minnesota has formal agreements, please visit the website for the Global Medical Education & Research Program.

The University of Minnesota Medical School uses the AAMC's Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) to receive visiting student applications.
To apply to our institution, you will need to complete an application in VSLO. Do not send elective requests directly to our office or to course personnel.

Do not email elective coordinators directly regarding elective availability or your application status.
Applications MUST conform the established UMN electives as listed in the VSLO elective catalog without exception.
Your applications will only be considered for the specific electives and the dates that you have applied for in VSLO.
Participation in an away rotation through the University of Minnesota Medical School does not guarantee an interview with the respective residency program.
Visiting students are only eligible to take a maximum of two (2) electives at our institution.

in good academic standing
completed at least 3 core clerkships (MED, SURG, OB/GYN, PED, Family Medicine and/or Psych) including one in the elective's specialty, if applicable
Any elective specific prerequisites will be listed on the catalog page for the elective.
passed USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX-USA Level 1
receiving credit for the elective at your home school
paying tuition at your home school during the period indicated
have certification in OSHA safety measures and infection control precautions
Current with Basic Life Support (BLS) training
ACLS will not be accepted in lieu of this requirement.

We accept only the AAMC Standard Immunization Form and require the following immunizations
Tuberculosis Screening - TB blood test (QuantiFERON TB-Gold or T-SPOT) OR Two-Step TST within one year of the elective end day. If positive, a normal chest x-ray dated after positive TB test is required.
Hepatitis B (documentation of vaccine or positive titer)
Measles, Mumps, Rubella (MMR) (documentation of two doses or positive titer for each)
Varicella (documentation of two doses OR positive titer OR provider diagnosis of disease date within one year of elective)
Tetanus/Diptheria/Pertussis Tdap (documentation of one dose received July 2005 or later and then 1 every 10 years after that)
Annual seasonal influenza vaccine
completed HIPAA training
professional liability and malpractice coverage is provided by your home school of at least 1,000,000 per instance and 3,000,000 aggregate

Assistant Registrar / Visiting Student/VSLO Coordinator: vsas@umn.edu
Shannon Benson — International Visiting Student Coordinator: benson@umn.edu

title: Penn Medicine (UPenn)
shortDescription: Ivy League academic medical center with strong clinical and research programs. Well-structured observer program with dedicated support for international physicians.
fullDescription:
Visiting Clerkship and Mentorship Program
The Visiting Clerkship Program encompasses numerous opportunities for 4th year medical students, and seeks to address some of the obstacles that can limit access to medical training. Students are invited to explore rotations at the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP), the [Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania, and Pennsylvania Hospital].

The clerkship includes both sub-internship and elective experiences in top-ranked residency programs. Students not only have the opportunity to spend time learning first-hand what it is like to be a resident at these institutions, but they are paired with resident and/or faculty mentors and meet with Residency Program Leadership to aid in their recruitment and preparation for the interview season and Match process. Stipends are included.

title: Duke University Hospital
shortDescription: Top-ranked academic medical center in the Southeast. Excellent clinical training environment with strong research opportunities. Known for cardiology and oncology.
fullDescription:
Duke School of Medicine will release the 2026-2027 Elective Book in VSLO on February 11th. Once this is released, students can submit applications. The deadline to submit an application will be April 10th. Students should apply early as some programs may decide before the deadline. This is for all terms during the 2026-2027 Academic Year. Students should also review the program website they are applying to for specific requirements and deadlines. All Visiting Students must follow our calendar. For questions, email Scott Campbell at scott.campbell@duke.edu.

The Visiting Medical Student Program benefits both Duke University School of Medicine and visiting students. Applicants must be in good academic standing, receive academic credit from their home school, and be progressing toward a Doctor of Medicine or Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree at an accredited institution. Eligible international institutions include Duke NUS, Heidelberg University School of Medicine, Nagoya University, and American University of Beirut. All requests must be approved by the Visiting Student Coordinator, and students must follow the Duke School of Medicine academic calendar.

title: UNC Hospitals
shortDescription: University of North Carolina's teaching hospital. Strong public medical school with excellent training. Research Triangle location offers academic collaboration opportunities.
fullDescription:
The University of North Carolina School of Medicine's Visiting Student Program offers domestic students in their final year of medical school the opportunity to participate in educational, engaging and challenging clinical elective experiences. We offer rotations with affiliated clinical sites and hospitals throughout North Carolina in cities such as Asheville, Chapel Hill, Charlotte, Greensboro, Raleigh, and Wilmington that represent a variety of different healthcare systems.

Must be currently enrolled in an American Medical School and be eligible to complete away rotations by home institution
Must have completed core clerkships
This includes: Family Medicine, Internal Medicine Obstetrics and Gynecology, Pediatrics, Psychiatry, Surgery
Must have passed USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX-USA Level 1
Must be in good academic standing at your home institution
Must have a current background clearance from your home institution

title: Wake Forest Baptist Medical Center
shortDescription: Academic medical center associated with Wake Forest School of Medicine. Strong cancer center and regenerative medicine programs. Smaller city with lower cost of living.
fullDescription:
The Visiting Medical Student Program at Wake Forest University School of Medicine provides an enriching opportunity for the school, health system and the applicant. Prioritization is given to Wake Forest University School of Medicine students. Visiting medical students who request a rotation are not guaranteed a rotation and rotations are subject to change.
To attend an elective rotation at the school of medicine, you must be actively pursuing a Doctor of Medicine or Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree, be in good academic standing, have successfully completed your 3rd year, and successfully passed USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX Level 1. All applicants must apply in the AAMC VSLO platform where additional requirements are noted.

All visiting student fees and payments are non-refundable and non-transferable. Cancellation or withdrawals must be made at least 5 weeks prior to the clinical rotation start date. Medical students must withdraw their application in VSLO.

title: Emory University Hospital
specialty: Internal Medicine, Infectious Disease, Cardiology, Transplant
shortDescription: Leading academic center with CDC partnership. Excellent for infectious disease and public health. Strong IMG-friendly culture with diverse training environment.
fullDescription:
MD and DO Students Currently Attending School in the U.S. and Puerto Rico
Fourth year MD and DO students attending AAMC Member Medical schools in the United States and Puerto Rico can apply through AAMC.
A non-refundable $100 application fee, payable through VSLO, will be required.
If accepted, students must pay an additional registration fee of $260 to Emory University.

MD Students Currently Attending International Medical Schools
Students who currently attend an international medical school and are fourth year medical students can apply directly to Emory University School of Medicine using the link below.
IF YOU ARE NOT A CURRENT UNITED STATES PASSPORT OR VISA HOLDER, PLEASE CHECK THE UNITED STATES VISA ENTRY REQUIREMENTS FOR YOUR COUNTRY BEFORE SUBMITTING YOUR APPLICATION.
The TOEFL iBT is required - the minimum score is 20, with no less than 5 scored on each individual section (no exceptions).
Please note that a non-refundable $525 application fee will be required. We accept applications from April 15, 2026 to May 15, 2026 from affiliated schools only. Students from non-affiliated schools can apply between May 16, 2026 and June 30, 2026 only.
International students are placed in electives only during the months of October to February.

page_excerpts.text:
Sinai Grace Hospital
Harper/Hutzel Hospital
Detroit Receiving Hospital
Children's Hospital
Rehabilitation Institute of Michigan

A current affiliation agreement between your school and the Detroit Medical Center is required. Please contact your school to find out if an affiliation agreement in in place. If there is not an affiliation agreement in place, you must apply through VSLO Visiting Student Learning Opportunities

Applications for elective rotations must be submitted no later than 60 days in advance of the requested start date. Any application received after the 60 days will not be considered.
Elective request must be on DMC's elective application. The application must be sent to the email address on the application electiverequests@dmc.org. Please DO NOT carbon copy anyone on the email.

Once approved for the rotation, students will receive an onboarding link from New Innovations. Students should start the onboarding process as soon as you receive the link. Failure to complete the onboarding process 31 days before the start of the rotation will result in the rotation being cancelled.

Visiting students may complete a total of 2 rotations at our sites, depending on availability.
Any cancellations or changes to scheduled rotations must be made at least 30 days before the rotation start date. Any cancellations or changes after 30 days will not be honored.
Failure to show up for a rotation is considered unprofessional behavior and we reserve the right to cancel any future rotations for that student.
At this time we do not accept International Medical Students.

All pediatric rotations, neurosurgery, trauma surgery and surgery intensive care and PM&R are 4 week rotations and are reserved for 4th year medical students.
PM&R only accepts students with a genuine interest in the program for residency. In addition to completing the elective application, students must also submit a statement of interest along with their application.
Our anesthesiology department does not take students in June and July. Radiology does not take students in December.

auditData.title: Barnes-Jewish Hospital (WashU)  [MO · St. Louis] (MISLABEL — VSLO ELECTIVE, observerships EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED; both US + global via VSLO network)

page_excerpts.text:
Washington University School of Medicine offers students in their final year of medical school the opportunity to participate in elective rotations at Barnes-Jewish Hospital, St. Louis Children's Hospital, and other affiliated clinical sites.
Washington University only accepts applications from prospective visiting medical students through AAMC's Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) platform.
Prospective students should NOT contact WUSM departments or faculty directly, as all visiting medical student enrollment is coordinated by the Electives Office.

Participation in an away rotation at Washington University School of Medicine does not guarantee that a student will be offered an interview with the respective residency program at WUSM.
WUSM faculty are not obligated to provide letters of recommendation for visiting medical students, and they may determine that a single four-week rotation does not provide sufficient evaluative information to write a strong letter of recommendation.

Medical students are NOT allowed to participate in hands-off observerships or shadowing.
Refer to the WashU Medicine Visitor Guidelines for more information.

Eligible students may only participate in one elective rotation at WUSM (maximum four weeks). This includes any elective rotation that may have been completed in a prior academic year. Students who have had an offer revoked by WUSM in a prior year may not be eligible for consideration in subsequent application cycles.

Must be currently enrolled in an eligible medical school.
Eligible medical schools include all institutions in the VSLO network (domestic and global).
Must have completed at least one year of required clinical training.
Must have completed the following core clerkships, with at least four weeks of training in each: Internal Medicine, Obstetrics & Gynecology, Pediatrics, Psychiatry, Surgery, and either Family Medicine or Neurology.
Must be in good academic standing and approved by the home institution to receive academic credit for the away rotation.
Must provide evidence of English language proficiency:
Evidence that the student is enrolled in medical school in the United States.
Evidence that the student is enrolled in a medical school where English is the primary language of instruction.
TOEFL score (minimum of 100; no other language tests are accepted).

The student will be covered by sufficient medical liability insurance during the dates of their rotation at WUSM.
Medical liability insurance coverage must be at least $1 million per instance and $3 million aggregate.
The student holds personal health insurance that is valid in the United States.
The student has successfully completed a criminal background check.
This student has successfully passed a drug test that screens for amphetamines, cannabinoids, cocaine, methamphetamines, opiates, and phencyclidine.

Current medical school transcript
AAMC Standardized Immunization Form, plus supplemental documentation:
TB skin or blood test that will be no more than 12 months old by the end of the latest requested rotation end date

page_excerpts.text:
Applications for 2026-2027 are accepted beginning March 15, 2026, and students will be notified as departments determine availability.
We are only accepting visiting students from U.S. accredited LCME and COCA medical schools.
Students must have completed the third-year core clerkships before their visiting rotation begins.
We are not accepting international student applications.
We do not accept visiting students in required third-year clerkships.

We prefer students follow our block schedule; however, the following departments will allow variable start and end dates based on current enrollment and staffing:
Anesthesiology
Ophthalmology
Orthopedic Surgery
Physical Medicine and Rehab

The University of Missouri uses VSLO, the Visiting Student Learning Opportunities Program, to receive all visiting student applications. For more information, please contact VSAS at vsas@aamc.org or 202-478-9878.
To apply to our school, complete an application on VSLO for your preferred electives and dates. Incomplete applications WILL NOT be accepted.

Visiting students are required to have had a mandatory background check and a seven-panel drug screen within the last three years.
We require the following documentation:
University of Missouri Mandatory Human Resources Form
We use Clinician Nexus to vet visiting medical students. You will need the following tests completed:
Nationwide Criminal Background Check within the last three years
Office of Inspector General (OIG)
SAM.GOV
State of MO Caregiver Screen
7-Panel Drug Screen
The AAMC Standardized Immunization Form
The Access and Confidentiality Agreement
Proof of BLS or ACLS certification
Passing of STEP or COMPLEX score
Official transcript
CV
Professional photo on a white or neutral background
Affiliation Agreements are required for all visiting medical students attending the University of Missouri.

Malpractice insurance of at least $3 million aggregate and $1 million per instance OR confirmation you will purchase supplemental insurance if your institution does not provide enough
Completed infection control, HIPPA, code of conduct, and PowerChart training modules.
If rotating between Oct. 1 and April 30, you will need to provide proof of a current flu shot

title: Indiana University Health
shortDescription: Indiana's largest academic medical center. Strong transplant and oncology programs. Affordable Midwest city. Good for IMGs seeking less competitive programs with quality training.
fullDescription:
Medical students enrolled at qualifying schools in the United States and Puerto Rico are eligible to apply for guest student elective study at Indiana University School of Medicine. MD or DO students interested in taking electives at IU School of Medicine can visit the AAMC/VSLO website for information and how to apply.
IU School of Medicine also offers the Pathway to Indiana Visiting Elective program for fourth year medical students and qualified third year medical students attending a U.S. medical school including U.S. territories. Please visit the program website for more details and how to apply.

In addition to the VSLO application, guest students must submit the following application materials at some point during the process:
Immunization Form — IU School of Medicine only accepts the AAMC Standardized Immunization Form, no exceptions.
Test Scores — Applicants must upload a copy of their USMLE STEP 1 or COMLEX LEVEL 1 board score. Some IU School of Medicine departments will not review guest student applications without this information.
Application Processing Fee — An application processing fee of $60 is due upon the guest student's acceptance of an elective offer.

page_excerpts.text:
Observerships
Opportunities for clinical and/or research observerships for visiting medical personnel are available.
It is the observer's responsibility to identify a medical staff host. No patient care or 'hands on' medical training will be conducted.

CLINICAL OBSERVERSHIPS
UW Medical Center: Apply to observe medical personnel at the UW Medical Center - Montlake and/or Northwest campus.
Harborview Medical Center: Medical personnel who wish to observe medical staff in clinical or research settings at HMC need to request observation privileges.

PRE-MED STUDENTS
Visit the School of Medicine website to set up a shadowing opportunity and apply.

INTERNATIONAL MEDICAL GRADUATES
Please note that we do not offer observerships for international medical graduates. UW has a robust medical student and resident educational training program. Because we are full with our own learners, we are unable to accommodate requests for shadowing, observations, or audition electives for International Medical Graduates.

auditData.title: Banner University Medical Center / University of Arizona  [AZ · Tucson]

page_excerpts.text:
Visiting Medical Students
The University of Arizona College of Medicine – Tucson accepts visiting students from other accredited medical schools. Applications are made through the Visiting Student Application Service (VSAS). The College of Medicine – Tucson Electives Catalog opens for applications in April. Applications are processed and offers extended no more than eight weeks prior to the start date for each elective.
Eligibility
To be a visiting student at the University of Arizona, you must:
be in your fourth year of medical school at an LCME- or COCA-accredited program
attend a university that has an affiliation agreement with the College of Medicine – Tucson
have either BLS or ACLS training that has not expired
Some electives require a letter of recommendation. We highly recommend you upload a letter of recommendation when you are submitting your application to VSAS.

We are NOT able to accommodate students from medical schools located outside of the United States. Visiting Students must be seniors in a four-year curriculum when the clerkship is taken and must have completed the equivalent of Tulane's basic clerkships in internal medicine, surgery, pediatrics, family medicine, psychiatry, neurology, and obstetrics and gynecology.

NOTE ON NEUROLOGY: A student's home institution must verify on their VSLO verifications tab that they will have completed neurology before their Tulane rotation begins: the home institution must include start date, end date, and weeks of duration on the VSLO verifications tab. This is a requirement for all students from all schools and applies to students applying for a rotation in any specialty at Tulane. If your school does not have a neurology requirement, you may complete a neurology elective instead. There are no exceptions.

page_excerpts.text: Thank you for your interest in senior clinical training at Tulane University School of Medicine. Tulane is pleased to offer clinical clerkships to senior-year students of other U.S. medical schools, including member schools of the Association of American Medical Colleges. We are NOT able to accommodate students from medical schools located outside of the United States. Visiting Students must be seniors in a four-year curriculum when the clerkship is taken and must have completed the equivalent of Tulane's basic clerkships in internal medicine, surgery, pediatrics, family medicine, psychiatry, neurology, and obstetrics and gynecology.

NOTE ON NEUROLOGY: A student's home institution must verify on their VSLO verifications tab that they will have completed neurology before their Tulane rotation begins: the home institution must include start date, end date, and weeks of duration on the VSLO verifications tab. This is a requirement for all students from all schools and applies to students applying for a rotation in any specialty at Tulane. If your school does not have a neurology requirement, you may complete a neurology elective instead. There are no exceptions.

Students must have successfully completed core clerkships, and have passed USMLE Step 1 exams prior to application.
We will only consider students in or entering their fourth year of a US medical school.
ALL students (including DO students) must have their home institution verify that they have taken/are scheduled for a neurology rotation that will end BEFORE their Tulane rotation.
A $225 non-refundable processing fee per student per accepted rotation will be charged. Please note that you will not be registered for the rotation until this fee is received.

Rotations completed by visiting students do not carry credit within Tulane Medical School; therefore, visiting students are not eligible for the M.D. degree from Tulane University.
Applications from visiting students are processed after April 15.
Tulane University School of Medicine cannot accommodate requests to change the dates of rotations. Rotations will follow Tulane's published schedule.
Visiting students will not be provided medical records training or access.
The visiting student is not to be supported nor compensated by Tulane Medical School funds.
The visiting student must make his/her own provisions for room and board and parking.
Tulane University School of Medicine will publish our offerings for visiting students on VSLO on or around April 15, 2026.

please do not email unsolicited application materials or letters of interest. You must apply through VSLO, VSLO sends host institutions daily updates about applications, and your VSLO application is sufficient indication of your interest in a visiting rotation at Tulane. The volume of email from visiting students prevents responses to unsolicited materials and letters of interest.

The Dr. Ernest W. Kinchen, Jr. Visiting Student Clerkship Award — The Tulane Surgery Department is offering a scholarship for visiting 4th-year students who are underrepresented in medicine and are interested in pursuing a career in general surgery.

page_excerpts.text: The Vanderbilt Observational Experience (VOE) allows participants to shadow a Vanderbilt University Medical Center (VUMC) healthcare professional - or Preceptor - during their working shift. VOE participants can experience what's involved in a medical profe[ssion]...

Participation in VOE is an opportunity to
Gain an understanding of interdisciplinary teamwork among our expert staff
Observe healthcare professionals' interactions with patients (hands-on activities are not included)

Meet potential future professional colleagues!
Most of our participants have identified the healthcare professional they'd like to observe, which can expedite participation in the program.

Anesthesia, Cardiothoracic Surgery, Dermatology, Emergency Medicine, Family Medicine, Internal Medicine, Neurology, Neurological Surgery, Obstetrics and Gynecology, Ophthalmology, Orthopedics, Otolaryngology, Pathology, Pediatrics, Psychiatry, Radiology, Radiation Oncology, Surgery, Plastic Surgery and Urology.

Only courses listed in the elective catalog and in the application are eligible for enrollment.

There will be a $75 non-refundable acceptance fee due if the visiting student application is accepted. This fee will be due at the time of offer to secure your seat.
Living arrangements are the responsibility of the student.
All visiting students should be familiar with The Student Handbook.
We require four weeks minimum notice to drop an elective. When students violate this policy, their home school will be notified.

Only after conditional acceptance from the department will you complete the following steps.
Step 1: Complete the Visiting Student Elective Application

Block schedule dates are 4 weeks in length, and align with the CCOM calendar. Off-cycle dates are not available. 2026 start dates: 7/6, 8/3, 8/31, 9/28.
We cannot accept applications from D.O. students in Osteopathic programs except from Des Moines University on a limited basis.

On a limited basis, CCOM is piloting our ability to accept visiting applications from Osteopathic fourth year students at Des Moines University... For 2026, Des Moines University students are welcome to apply for clerkships in the departments of: Anesthesia, Cardiothoracic Surgery, Emergency Medicine, Neurosurgery, Pathology, Pediatrics, Psychiatry, Radiation Oncology, Radiology, Urology
Brief 1-2 day 'auditions' are not allowed.

foreign medical schools,
non-LCME accredited M.D. institutions,

Elective rotations are four (4) weeks in length and are offered each month of the academic year. The visiting student will be expected to adhere to the COM rotations schedule and be able to attend all four (4) weeks of the offered rotation. Only one (1) rotation can be accepted per visiting student.
The COM does not offer two (2) week elective rotations.

Only complete applications submitted through the VSLO application service will be reviewed for consideration. A complete application consists of the following:
CV Uploaded · Headshot Uploaded · Home Institution Transcript Uploaded · Immunizations Uploaded and verified by student health representation or physician · Transcript Uploaded · State and Federal Background Check uploaded
Required background check searches: Social Security Number Verification · Nationwide Federal Criminal · US Nationwide Criminal · Sex Offender Registry · Statewide Criminal · Adult and Child Abuse Registry · Office of Inspector General Sanctions

Anesthesiology: List of previous electives + brief statement about why UNMC
Emergency Medicine: USMLE Step 2 score (if available) + brief statement of interest
Internal Medicine: USMLE Step 2 score (if available) + brief statement about why UNMC
Neurosurgery: USMLE Step 2 score (if available)
OB/GYN: Brief statement about why UNMC

The UAB Heersink School of Medicine welcomes senior medical students interested in applying to the visiting student program. All students must be US citizens or permanent residents attending an LCME-accredited medical school or osteopathic school accredited by American Osteopathic Association (AOA). page_excerpts.text: Federal regulations prevent international observers from having patient care responsibilities or involvement in any form of volunteer research.
Visits are restricted to a maximum of one month.

**The International Observer Program at MSK is not designed for international medical graduates (IMG) residing in the United States or abroad, who intend to apply for residency or fellowship in the U.S. These applications will not be considered for this program.

Who is eligible?
Fully trained international physicians with several years of medical practice currently employed and practicing abroad.
Applicants currently in a fellowship program abroad may apply to the specialty relevant to their fellowship and will be required to submit proof of residency completion.
Residents must be in a specialized residency abroad and in their 3rd or 4th year in that program. Written confirmation from the program and medical school diploma are required to apply.
Recent medical graduates and junior residents (first- and second-year residents) are not eligible for the program and are not able to apply.
Medical students are not eligible for this program.

Contact an MSK faculty member to discuss visiting MSK – The International program does not match applicants with sponsors and is unable to give out faculty contact information"
"The approval process takes at least 4-6 weeks

auditData.title: University of Kansas Medical Center (KUMC) — International Observership  [KS · Kansas City]

page_excerpts.text: The International Observership Program is overseen by the Office of International Programs (OIP). Observers can be defined as visiting residents, physicians or medical school graduates.

Individuals participating in this program through the Office of International Programs are generally International Medical Graduates regardless of immigration status. Observers can be defined as visiting residents, physicians or medical school graduates. Current medical students are ineligible for observerships.

International observerships are limited to a four (4)-week visit. During this time, international observers will strictly observe patient treatment or clinical work while accompanied at all times by sponsoring supervisor or designated attending physician. International observers are not permitted to engage in any patient care or be left alone with patients. Individuals may observe in the same department as a family member, but cannot be directly supervised by a family member.
Contacting clinicians, researchers, and department personnel on your own outside of this process may result in your ineligibility for observerships.
The annual inquiry submission window is 15 October-15 December. Requests after 15 December will not be processed.

If you decide to continue with the application process despite no guarantee of placement in a program, note that a fee of $50 USD will be assessed for each program inquiry received. This fee is mandatory and non-refundable and doesn't guarantee a host. However, program inquiries will not be reviewed until payment is received.
IMPORTANT: The International Observership Program is a fee-based program. The International applicant is responsible for paying the fees to participate. The standard fee charged for the program is $3,000 for the month.

Pediatrics does not accept observers.
Obstetrics and Gynecology does not accept observers.
Pathology and Laboratory Medicine will not accept applicants in June or July.
Cardiovascular Medicine accepts two observers per month.

To apply for the 2026 cycle, please email iirpobserve@kumc.edu. This will prompt an automated response with the link to the inquiry application which should be filled out for consideration.
When inquiring for the online survey, please do not attach any documents.

auditData.title: Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) International Observership

page_excerpts.text: The International Observership Program offers eligible international colleagues the opportunity to observe patient care in a healthcare setting without patient contact for the specific purpose of gaining professional skills, knowledge, and attitudes. CHOP international observerships are customized to meet the needs and requests of the applicant, and last two to eight weeks. Longer observerships are accommodated on a case-by-case basis.
There is a one-time $750 administrative fee and background screening fee which varies depending on the country of origin. A tuition fee may also apply.

To be eligible for observership, applicants must be fluent in English and be one of the following:
A fully trained healthcare professional currently working abroad, intending to return to his/her position abroad.
An international medical fellow or resident currently enrolled in a fellowship/residency program at a foreign institution, intending to return to that institution for graduation.
An international student studying a health-related field who has not yet received his/her terminal degree, intending to return to a country outside the United States to practice his/her healthcare field.
Medical students seeking hands-on clinical electives for credit are not eligible for this program and should explore opportunities through Penn Global Health Programs.
CHOP observerships must not be used to obtain US-based experience for the purpose of seeking graduate medical training (residency and fellowship) in the United States.

Given the observational nature of this experience, where participants cannot perform hands-on clinical care, CHOP faculty cannot provide letters of recommendation or complete evaluation forms for participants. However, observers will receive a certificate of attendance for the dates of the observership.

title: Boston Children's Hospital — Observership
shortDescription: Observership for fully trained physicians or actively training residents/fellows. Medical students referred to HMS Clerkship Exchange. Must be fluent in English.
fullDescription:
The Observership Program at Boston Children's provides knowledge of best practices to improve pediatric care worldwide and promotes a collaborative environment for the exchange of ideas. Observerships are informal learning experiences and do not constitute formal training or patient contact. They are offered to health care providers across a number of departments and can range from one to 12 weeks, although most programs limit the duration to four weeks.
As part of the curriculum, observers may shadow clinical activities and procedures and attend daily rounds, lectures, seminars, and other interdisciplinary conferences in the hospital.

auditData.title: Cincinnati Children's Hospital — International Visitor Program

page_excerpts.text: Every year, Cincinnati Children's hosts non-employee international visitors throughout the institution. These health professionals can apply to come in a non-employee capacity in one of two ways: clinical observers and researchers.

To be eligible for the International Visitor Program at Cincinnati Children's, applicants must either be on staff at an abroad institution or in an accredited program during time of application and during the requested time of visit.
There is no educational credit or certificate for coming to Cincinnati Children's.

Clinical Observerships
Applicants interested in a clinical observership must meet one of the following requirements:
Be a current attending physician, resident, or fellow abroad;
Hold a PhD or Master's Degree and be employed abroad; or
Be enrolled in a PhD program abroad

Observers are eligible to enter the US on a B-1/B-2 visa or through the Visa Waiver Program (if the visitor is a citizen of a country approved for the Visa Waiver Program). Observerships are usually between one week and three months in length, but vary by division.
International visiting clinical observers are allowed to come to Cincinnati Children's to observe only (i.e., hands-on training and diagnoses are prohibited).

Cincinnati Children's is authorized to host J-1 visitors in two categories: Research Scholar and Short-Term Scholar.
J-1 Scholars must meet one of these criteria:
Have earned a PhD or MD
Have earned a master's degree or be enrolled in a master's degree program in a field related to their work at Cincinnati Children's.
Have earned a bachelor's degree and have minimally three years of relevant research experience.

UofL Health retains the right to end a MOP rotation if a violation of expectations occurs

page_excerpts.text: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Clinical Observership Program aims to acculturate International Medical Graduates to American medical practices and help prepare them for residency

The program follows the American Medical Association International Medical Graduates (AMA-IMG) section observership guidelines. This program is based on the Oklahoma State Medical Association's IMG observership and the American Association of Physicians of Indian Origin observership program. SAMS offers this non-for-profit program in several states.

Interested candidates can apply by filling out the SAMS Observership Application and providing the necessary documentation. Applications will be reviewed by the SAMS Observership Committee and applicants will be accepted for a 1-2 month rotation upon approval. By the end of the observership, both preceptors and observers are required to submit an evaluation form to the observership committee.

SAMS will not pay a salary or stipend to the observers.
SAMS will consider supporting the candidate to pay the program fees, however, lodging, boarding, and transportation are the responsibility of the observer.
Depending on the performance of the observer, he/she might obtain a Letter of Recommendation from the preceptor.
All observers will be awarded a Certificate of Completion upon the completion of the observership.

Must be a graduate of an international medical school.
Must hold a valid U.S. visa, green card, temporary protection status (TPS), or be an American citizen.
Passing at least one of the United States Medical Licensing Examinations (USMLE) Step 1, Step 2 CK, or Step 2 CS. However, few programs might require an ECFMG certificate at the time of application.
Send at least 1 reference letter by a physician practicing in the U.S. This can be a resident physician.
Be proficient in English.

There are no fees for SAMS Observership applications; however, some institutions require fees for the observership. The applicant will be responsible for these fees and has to pay them after acceptance into an observership position.

Completed SAMS observership application form
Copy of a valid U.S. visa
Copy of medical school diploma and medical school transcript
USMLE scores and ECFMG certificate if available.

auditData.title: Georgetown / Ruesch Center — International GI Observership  [DC · Washington]

page_excerpts.text:
International Clinical Observership Program Gastrointestinal Division (Ruesch Center / Georgetown Lombardi Comprehensive Cancer Center)
The Ruesch Center for the Cure of Gastrointestinal Cancer at the Lombardi Comprehensive Cancer Center in Washington DC offers a medical observership program, designed for junior to mid-career physicians interested in gaining in depth knowledge of the treatment of gastrointestinal cancer, research, program building and patient care. The observership is a participant-tailored exchange program in which the observer accompanies the medical staff through their daily hospital and academic routines, sharing experiences with doctors, residents, research staff and students. This program is not for academic credit.

fullDescription:
IMG/IMS Observership
Step into the fascinating world of advanced neurosurgery, pain management and internal medicine, through our dynamic observership program for international medical graduates and medical students at MSK MSO, based in San Diego, California.
Whether you're a medical student or an experienced professional, this program offers the opportunity to observe cutting-edge procedures and gain valuable insights into the field.

Pricing: $1,500/4 weeks
Payment Policy: The total program fee is $1,500, which includes a $200 non-refundable registration fee. Full payment must be received no later than 6 weeks prior to the program start date.

Valid visa and medical credentials
Graduation credentials OR proof of medical studies
Background check
Vaccination records

Are international graduates eligible for admission into the internal medicine program?

page_excerpts.text: Our office helps to arrange clinical rotations for all students who are not part of the Internal Medicine Residency Program. Examples of programs include nursing, radiology, sterile processing, and laboratory science.

Please contact the Educational Center for Professional Development at 201-915-2948. Our office hours are 8AM-4PM, Monday through Friday, or you may leave a voicemail. We will collect some information about your desired clinical rotation, your school, and your degree program before proceeding. If appropriate, your request is then passed on to the relevant clinical department for verification that they wish to precept/supervise students.
Because some academic institutions may not yet have an academic affiliation agreement in place with JCMC, please allow 8-12 additional weeks for completion of this agreement.

The existence of an agreement between your school and JCMC does not guarantee the automatic approval of any student experiences. Experiences are granted based upon department and preceptor availability, in keeping with hospital standards and patient safety needs.

This paperwork needs to be on file with our office a minimum of 8-12 weeks prior to the requested start date.
The clinical faculty or student must also complete a mandatory online learning module before the start of the clinical.

page_excerpts.text: Thank you for your interest in pursuing senior electives at UT Southwestern Medical School and our UT Southwestern University Hospital.
If you attend a U.S. medical school, you must apply through Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) to a senior elective.

If you attend an international medical school, you must apply using our paper application. Review Visiting Medical Students from International Medical Schools for more information on requirements both before and after you've been accepted. This includes all U.S. citizens who are attending medical schools in other countries.

Payment
Visiting students that have been accepted by the department to complete a rotation at UT Southwestern must:
Pay a rotation fee prior to starting their rotation(s)
Either purchase or provide proof of malpractice insurance coverage prior to starting their rotation(s)

title: University of New Mexico Hospital
specialty: Internal Medicine, Family Medicine, Emergency Medicine, Psychiatry
shortDescription: New Mexico's only Level I trauma center and academic medical center. Serves a large rural and Native American population. Unique clinical exposure. Very affordable area.
fullDescription:
The University of New Mexico School of Medicine welcomes final-year visiting medical students to participate in four-week elective clerkships for credit. Clerkships are coordinated through the Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) program by the Office of Medical Student Affairs.
ALL visiting applicants must apply through the AAMC web-based VSLO application system. No exceptions can be made.

The School of Medicine requires all visiting students to meet the same standards expected of our own students. Per the Student Guidelines outlined in the UNM VSLO Course Catalog, visiting students may not seek clerkship 'pre-approval' from any UNM department.
eligibilitySummary:

page_excerpts.text:
The University of New Mexico School of Medicine welcomes final-year visiting medical students to participate in four-week elective clerkships for credit. Clerkships are coordinated through the Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) program by the Office of Medical Student Affairs.
ALL visiting applicants must apply through the AAMC web-based VSLO application system. No exceptions can be made.

The School of Medicine requires all visiting students to meet the same standards expected of our own students. Per the Student Guidelines outlined in the UNM VSLO Course Catalog, visiting students may not seek clerkship 'pre-approval' from any UNM department.

Only final-year medical students may apply.
Applications are open only to institutions within the VSLO system. Course availability varies by department.
All visiting students must apply for UNM electives and be eligible in VSLO. Please do not contact faculty or department administration directly to attempt to arrange rotations for credit.
Observerships, shadowing opportunities, research electives, or pre-clinical experiences are not offered.

All visiting students must have an affiliation agreement on file prior to the start of a rotation. Students are advised to not make non-refundable travel and lodging arrangements until a fully-executed affiliation agreement is in place.
All fees must be paid before a visiting student can be registered for their rotation. Students with unpaid fees 30 days out from a rotation start date will be dropped from the course.

auditData.title: University of Kansas Medical Center  [KS · Kansas City] (MISLABEL — VSLO ELECTIVE, Fall-only, currently CLOSED for AY26-27)

page_excerpts.text: Thank you for your interest in completing a rotation with us at the University of Kansas School of Medicine. Due to the number of applications already received for the Fall 2026 term for visiting student rotations, we have now formally closed our VSLO portal to new applications for the 26-27 AY and are working on processing the requests that we have received. At this time, we are no longer accepting new applications, and all students that have applied already via VSLO will be notified of the status of their applications. We now consider our spots available for outside students to be at capacity, so we will not be reviewing any new applications for the 26-27 academic year.

Visiting medical students may apply to any available elective at the KU School of Medicine and will be considered after KU medical students have been scheduled. Visiting Students are only accepted for the Fall term.

Away rotations will resume on June 1, 2026.
Students may begin applying for in-person away rotations on Friday, March 13, 2026 for rotations starting on June 1, 2026, or later.
The number of away rotations allowed is limited to one four-week rotation per student.
Accepted students will be required to be fitted for an N-95 Mask in advance of their rotation. KUMC cannot provide mask fittings.
Visiting students accepted for a rotation will be required to pay a nominal fee of approximately $110.00 once they receive a bill from Student Financial Accounting.
The KU School of Medicine Kansas City Campus utilizes the AAMC Visiting Student Learning Opportunities (VSLO) to receive visiting M.D. and D.O. student applications.

This year, KUMC opened the VSLO application process to three Osteopathic schools (Kansas City University; Des Moines University COOM; and Oklahoma State University COOM).
Due to the volume of applications and the processing time for each application submitted, it was decided to narrow our focus and limit the schools that are able to apply. Other Osteopathic Schools that use VLSO for the application system for the upcoming 2026-2027 AY year should not apply and will be ineligible for rotations.

title: NYU Langone International Observership — General and Medical Dermatology
shortDescription: Month-long observership in general and medical dermatology clinics at NYU Langone's Ronald O. Perelman Department.
fullDescription: This one-month observership places visiting physicians inside NYU Langone's Ronald O. Perelman Department of Dermatology, one of the largest academic dermatology departments in the United States. Participants rotate through subspecialty clinics at the Ambulatory Care Center on East 38th Street, sit in on daily morning didactics, grand rounds, and the Tuesday Night Conference, and spend time with faculty in private practice settings. The program admits one track per month September through June, and seats fill on a rolling basis. Observers attend in business-casual attire (black scrubs for procedural observation) and do not provide any direct patient care. Applicants must hold a medical degree, have completed formal dermatology training equivalent to a US residency, demonstrate English proficiency via TOEFL, and currently reside outside the United States. Entry requires a B-1 visa or visa waiver; NYU verifies visa status but does not sponsor J-1 visas. The application packet is substantial: a 500-600 word personal statement, CV, three physician references, medical school and residency diplomas, TOEFL proof, and government-issued photo ID, due at least three months before the requested start date. The department explicitly notes the observership does not satisfy American Board of Dermatology eligibility, is not a fellowship, and does not confer residency credit. A certificate of completion is issued at the end of the rotation.
eligibilitySummary: MD plus completed dermatology residency required. TOEFL required. Must reside outside the US. Not ABD-eligible; not positioned for US Match.

title: NYU Langone International Observership — Dermatologic Surgery and Cosmetics
shortDescription: Month-long surgical dermatology observership covering Mohs, cosmetic procedures, and facial reconstruction.
fullDescription: The Dermatologic Surgery and Cosmetics track places trained international dermatologists alongside NYU Langone's surgical and cosmetic faculty for one intensive month. Observers watch high-volume Mohs micrographic surgery, dermatologic oncology, facial reconstruction, and cosmetic work including neuromodulators, dermal fillers, and laser procedures. The program emphasizes surgical decision-making and technique rather than hands-on practice; observers never provide direct patient care. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Participants must already hold a medical degree, have completed dermatology training equivalent to a US residency, pass TOEFL, reside outside the US, and obtain a B-1 visa or visa waiver. The application packet mirrors the General track and is due three months ahead of start date. Fees are $6,000 per month, paid 50% on approval and 50% thirty days before arrival, with full refund available for cancellations at least 30 days prior. Days include subspecialty clinic time at the Ambulatory Care Center on East 38th Street, morning didactics, grand rounds, and the Tuesday Night Conference. A certificate is issued on completion. Like the General track, this rotation does not count toward American Board of Dermatology eligibility and is not structured for IMGs preparing for US residency Match.
eligibilitySummary: MD plus completed dermatology residency required. TOEFL required. Must reside outside the US. Not ABD-eligible; not a Match pathway.

title: NYU Langone International Observership — Hair Disorders
shortDescription: Focused month-long observership in hair loss and scalp disorder evaluation at NYU Langone Dermatology.
fullDescription: The Hair Observership is the most narrowly scoped of NYU Langone's three international dermatology tracks. It places a trained dermatologist alongside faculty who specialize in hair and scalp disorders for one month, with days spent observing patient evaluation and management for conditions like androgenetic alopecia, alopecia areata, scarring alopecias, and other trichology concerns. Observers do not perform or assist with patient care. Tuition is not posted publicly and must be requested directly from the program coordinators. Eligibility matches the other two tracks: medical degree, completed dermatology residency (or equivalent subspecialty training), TOEFL proof, residence outside the United States, and a B-1 visa or visa waiver. The application packet is due at least three months before the requested start date. A certificate of completion is issued. The rotation does not qualify the observer for American Board of Dermatology pathways and is not positioned as a stepping stone to US residency. It is best suited to already-trained dermatologists who want focused exposure to NYU's hair-disorder practice before returning home.
eligibilitySummary: MD plus completed dermatology residency required. TOEFL required. Specialty visiting, not IMG Match track.
