title: Mayo Clinic Visiting Medical Student Clerkship
specialty: Multi-specialty (IM, Surgery, Peds, Radiology, Neurology, Anesthesia, EM, OB/GYN, Psychiatry, Pathology)
shortDescription: Flagship four-week sub-internship-style clerkship at Mayo Clinic's three campuses via VSLO-only pathway for US and international senior students.
fullDescription: Mayo Clinic's Visiting Medical Student Clerkship is one of the most structured visiting elective programs in US academic medicine. Rotations are offered in nearly every clinical department across the Rochester (MN), Phoenix-Scottsdale (AZ), and Jacksonville (FL) campuses, including internal medicine and subspecialties, surgical disciplines, pediatrics, neurology, anesthesiology, radiology, pathology, psychiatry, emergency medicine, and obstetrics-gynecology. Every clerkship is exactly four weeks long, begins on a Monday, and ends on a Friday four weeks later. Applications are accepted only through AAMC VSLO. Senior medical students at LCME- or COCA-accredited US schools can apply to all three campuses, while Rochester and Phoenix-Scottsdale also accept students from international medical schools (Jacksonville is US-only). US applicants typically submit one LOR; international applicants submit two. Dean's letter or equivalent home-school verification is required. USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX Level 1 passing documentation is required, and core clerkships must be complete before the start date. The program is widely used as a pre-application touchpoint by students preparing for Mayo's residency programs.
cost: Admin fees vary by campus; no tuition for US LCME/COCA students; international applicants pay higher processing fees
eligibilitySummary: Final-year student at LCME/COCA US school OR approved international school (Rochester + Arizona only). Core clerkships complete. Step 1 or COMLEX Level 1 pass.
stepRequirements: USMLE Step 1 pass (or COMLEX Level 1)
adminNotes:
Verified 2026-04-17. Mayo 3-campus flagship. Tier A homepage feature.

title: University of Michigan Visiting Medical Student Program
specialty: Multi-specialty; includes Health Equity Visiting Clerkship
shortDescription: Clinical electives at Michigan Medicine via VSLO with a 45-day-out deadline. Includes the Health Equity Visiting Clerkship.
fullDescription: The University of Michigan Medical School welcomes final-year visiting medical students for clinical electives at Michigan Medicine. A maximum of 8 weeks may be completed, research electives are not permitted, and only electives listed in the VSLO visiting catalog are available. The application deadline is 45 days before rotation start. International applicants are directed to Michigan's Global REACH office. The Health Equity Visiting Clerkship (HEVC) is a companion four-week rotation for final-year students interested in academic medicine and improving care for underserved populations, combining clinical exposure with mentorship, career development, and community engagement. U-M also runs dedicated visiting sub-internships in Orthopaedic Surgery, Plastic Surgery, and Dermatology with separate departmental guidance.
duration: Up to 8 weeks total; no research electives
cost: UMMS admin fees; no tuition for enrolled US students

title: University of Pittsburgh Visiting Medical Student Program
shortDescription: Clinical electives at Pitt/UPMC for 4th-year US and select international students. URiM-focused clerkships include stipend support.
fullDescription: The University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine accepts visiting medical students through two pathways: a domestic track for 4th-year students at LCME- or AOA-accredited North American medical schools, and a limited international track for students from outside North America. Applications are submitted through AAMC VSLO and administered via the Office of Student Affairs. Pitt also runs two flagship equity-focused rotations: the Jeannette E. South-Paul Visiting Clerkship (JSPVC), a one-month experience for URiM senior medical students that pays a $2,000 travel/living stipend, and the Carey Andrew-Jaja, MD Visiting Elective Clerkship Program, which is OB/GYN-specific at UPMC Magee-Womens Hospital. Affiliation agreement, malpractice coverage, and immunizations must be in place before start. Rotations are regarded as unofficial auditions for Pitt residencies.

title: Northwestern Feinberg Visiting Students Program
specialty: Multi-specialty at NMH, Lurie Children's, Shirley Ryan AbilityLab
shortDescription: Eight-week rotation blocks at Northwestern Memorial and affiliates for 4th-year (and qualified 3rd-year) students. 2026-2027 applications opened March 9, 2026.
fullDescription: Feinberg's Visiting Students Program receives thousands of applications each year for a limited number of clerkship seats on its Streeterville campus, home to Northwestern Memorial Hospital, Lurie Children's Hospital, and the Shirley Ryan AbilityLab. The domestic track is open to 4th-year students at LCME/AOA-accredited US schools, plus 3rd-year students who have completed core clerkships in medicine, surgery, pediatrics, and OB/GYN. International students may apply only from select Global Partner institutions with active exchange agreements. All applications are processed through AAMC VSLO with the AAMC Standardized Immunization Form; rotations are offered in 8-week blocks. The 2026-2027 cycle opened March 9, 2026. Feinberg additionally runs the McGaw Visiting Clerkship Stipend for US-citizen or permanent-resident students approved for AAMC Fee Assistance Program and interested in community health — up to $1,000 for rotations at NMH/Shirley Ryan and up to $2,000 at Lurie Children's.

title: University of Chicago Pritzker Visiting Students Program
specialty: Medicine (8w), Surgery (8w), Peds, OB-GYN, Psychiatry + subspecialty electives
shortDescription: Domestic VSLO pathway at UChicago for final-year LCME/COCA students. 2026-27 catalog publishes March 2, applications open March 9, 2026.
fullDescription: Pritzker School of Medicine hosts final-year domestic medical students from LCME- or COCA-accredited schools for clinical elective rotations. Applications are accepted only through AAMC VSLO; the 2026-27 catalog publishes March 2, 2026, and applications open March 9, 2026. Most rotations are four weeks (with 8-week options in medicine and surgery), running from the first working Monday of the month. Students can complete up to eight weeks total at Pritzker, and placement is first-come-first-served with a six-week-in-advance application recommendation. A strict malpractice requirement of $1M per instance and $3M aggregate is enforced without exception. International students are admitted only if enrolled at 'US-affiliated medical schools located in other countries' through the Center for Global Health. Fees are minimal — no tuition for up to 8 weeks, a $26 wellness fee, parking at $28/day. Students cannot rotate in required clerkships; electives only.
cost: No tuition up to 8 weeks; $26 wellness fee; $1M/$3M malpractice required
eligibilitySummary: 4th-year MD/DO at LCME/COCA US schools; specific US-affiliated international schools via Center for Global Health.
stepRequirements: Departments may require Step 1 passage
adminNotes:
Verified 2026-04-17. Very low fees — $26 wellness only.

page_excerpts.text: Visiting Students | Pritzker School of Medicine | The University of Chicago Academics Visiting Students Fourth-year medical students who have successfully completed all of their core clerkships and who are from schools fully-accredited* by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education (LCME) or the Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation (COCA) may participate in visiting student electives offered at the University of Chicago Pritzker School of Medicine. Visiting students may participate in fourth-year electives only. Most rotations are four weeks long and begin either the first day of the month (unless this day falls on a weekend) or on the first working Monday of the month. All Department of Medicine rotations start on the first day of the month (unless it is a weekend day) except for 2-week rotations that may begin mid-month on date specified in the course catalog. *Rotation dates are non-negotiable and cannot be altered. They have been meticulously arranged and coordinated to align precisely with our program's requirements and available resources. 2026-27 PSOM Course Catalog The ’26–’27 Course Catalog will be available in VSLO on Monday, March 2, 2026. Applications will open for submission on Monday, March 9, 2026. If you have a question, please reference the Frequently Asked Questions Page before contacting our office. title: Washington University St. Louis Visiting Medical Student Electives
specialty: Multi-specialty at Barnes-Jewish and St. Louis Children's
shortDescription: Single 4-week elective for final-year US students at Barnes-Jewish and St. Louis Children's via VSLO with a flat $100 admin fee.
fullDescription: The Washington University School of Medicine Electives Office hosts final-year medical students for clinical elective rotations at Barnes-Jewish Hospital, St. Louis Children's Hospital, and other affiliated clinical sites. Applications run exclusively through AAMC VSLO; home medical schools must be VSLO network members. Because of very high application volume, eligible students may participate in only one four-week rotation at WashU Medicine, and even eligible applicants are not guaranteed placement. Tuition is not charged; accepted students pay a flat $100 administration fee to confirm their spot. Observerships and shadowing are not offered, and faculty are not obligated to provide letters of recommendation, though LORs are possible. Direct contact with departments or faculty is discouraged before conditional acceptance.
cost: No tuition; $100 admin fee upon acceptance

title: University of Wisconsin–Madison Visiting Medical Student Program
shortDescription: VSLO visiting electives at UW Health restricted to US citizens, Canadian citizens, or US permanent residents at LCME/AOA schools.
fullDescription: The University of Wisconsin School of Medicine and Public Health offers visiting medical students clinical electives across its departments via AAMC VSLO. Eligibility is notably strict: applicants must be US citizens, Canadian citizens, or US permanent residents, currently enrolled at an LCME- or AOA-accredited medical school, in their final year of training at the time of rotation, and have passed USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX Level 1 — 'no exceptions.' VSLO does not accept international medical students. Standard timing is mid-March catalog publication, mid-April application opening, and mid-May review start. fullDescription: University of Iowa Carver College of Medicine runs its visiting student elective program outside the AAMC VSLO platform: UIHC does not participate in VSLO. Instead, applicants use the Iowa application portal, which opens April 1 for 2026 opportunities. The 2026 block start dates are July 6, August 3, August 31, and September 28. Eligibility is 4th-year medical students from LCME-accredited schools only — DO students are generally ineligible except for a limited pilot for Des Moines University students. Requirements include completion of core clinical courses comparable to Carver's curriculum, USMLE Step 1 passage, health and malpractice insurance ($1M/$3M minimum), HIPAA and universal-precautions training, completed background check, and immunization compliance. Some departments require a separate departmental application first, followed by CCOM institutional steps after conditional acceptance. A $75 non-refundable acceptance fee is due upon offer.

shortDescription: VSLO electives + audition rotations at Henry Ford Health campuses. 2026 applications opened Feb 9, 2026. One elective + one audition per cycle.
fullDescription: Henry Ford Health hosts visiting medical students for clinical electives and audition rotations through AAMC VSLO across its Detroit campus and satellites in Wyandotte, Jackson, Macomb, and Warren. Non-base-clerkship students must apply via VSLO unless they attend one of the Primary Academic Partner schools (MSUCOM, KCOM, CMU). 2026 applications opened February 9, 2026, and rotation start dates run from August 3, 2026 through June 1, 2027. Each visiting student may participate in one elective rotation (for exploration or additional clinical experience) and one audition rotation (aimed at a specific residency program). A $125 administrative fee per elective is charged at Henry Ford Hospital Detroit; Henry Ford Warren requires a refundable $75 processing fee paid only by money order. Personal health insurance is mandatory, and students must produce full immunization documentation.
cost: $125 admin fee at Detroit; $75 refundable fee at Warren (money order only)
eligibilitySummary: Medical students at accredited US schools applying via VSLO (or Primary Academic Partner schools directly); full immunization + insurance required.

title: NIH Medical Student Summer Opportunities to Advance Research (M-SOAR)
specialty: Translational biomedical research with clinical exposure
shortDescription: Full-time summer research placement at NIH Bethesda for first-year US medical students with translational research and clinical exposure.
fullDescription: M-SOAR is a targeted track within the NIH Summer Internship Program (SIP) for first-year medical students interested in translational research. Students spend the summer embedded in one of the NIH Institutes and Centers in the Intramural Research Program, working on the main Bethesda campus (Maryland) under the direct mentorship of a Principal Investigator. Experiences span basic, translational, and clinical research, with the clinical exposure particularly rich at the NIH Clinical Center. Applications are reviewed by individual PIs rather than centrally — prospective students are expected to identify and contact PIs directly starting in early January, and selection typically completes by April 1. Eligibility requires US citizenship or permanent residency, enrollment at least half-time at an accredited US institution, and medical-student status.
eligibilitySummary: First-year US medical students at accredited US schools; US citizen or permanent resident; half-time enrollment.

title: MSK Medical Student Summer Research Fellowship
specialty: Cancer research (lab, clinical, biostats, epi) with clinical shadowing
shortDescription: Eight-week MSK summer fellowship for 1st and 2nd year medical students combining cancer research with clinical shadowing.
fullDescription: The MSK Medical Student Summer Research Fellowship is a structured 8-week program for medical students between their first and second years of study. The 2026 program runs June 15, 2026 through August 7, 2026. Fellows select from laboratory bench research, clinical research, biostatistics, and epidemiology projects under the direct mentorship of MSK faculty. Beyond the research project, the curriculum layers in clinical shadowing rotations with oncology teams, didactic sessions on oncology and research methodologies, professional development, and communication skill-building focused on conveying research findings. The program is widely regarded as one of the strongest early-career pipelines into oncology for medical students, with many alumni proceeding to match into MSK or peer oncology residency programs.
eligibilitySummary: 1st or 2nd year medical students in good standing at accredited medical schools.

title: Stanford Visiting Clerkship Program
specialty: EM, Surgery, Medicine, Radiology, Anesthesia, Peds
shortDescription: Stanford moved fully onto VSLO for AY 2026-2027. Summer-quarter applications opened Feb 13, 2026. Strict 4-week cap.
fullDescription: Stanford Medicine's Visiting Clerkship Program migrated entirely to the AAMC VSLO platform beginning with the 2026-2027 academic year. Summer-quarter applications opened February 13, 2026; later quarters open on a staggered schedule through the year. Visiting MD and DO students can apply for a single four-week rotation in a department participating in that quarter, and Stanford strictly caps visitors at one four-week block. Complete applications must include a personal statement, letters of recommendation, a CV, and an official medical-school transcript. After a rotation offer is extended, students must complete post-decision requirements including proof of health insurance, a criminal background check within two years of the rotation end date, and an N95 respirator mask fit-test within twelve months. Stanford does not guarantee housing. The Stanford Clinical Opportunity for Residency Experience (SCORE) Program runs as a parallel pathway providing additional mentorship and financial support.
cost: VSLO fee + Stanford registration and health-screening costs
eligibilitySummary: MD/DO student in final year at LCME or COCA school; full application through VSLO.
stepRequirements: Dept-specific; Step 1 pass typically required

Visiting MD & DO Students
Stanford Medicine is currently offering a maximum of 4-week elective rotation/clerkship to MD or DO students who will be enrolled in their fourth or final year at a school accredited by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education or Education Department of the American Osteopathic Association.

fullDescription: The University of Colorado School of Medicine's Extern/Visiting Student Program opens its VSLO catalog and application for the 2026-2027 academic year on March 2, 2026. Rotation blocks are four weeks long, with a single three-week block September 21 to October 11, and run May through early November. VSLO is available to final-year medical students from LCME-accredited US schools and COCA-accredited osteopathic schools. Completed applications must be on file at least eight weeks before the course start date. International visiting students are handled through a separate pathway and limited to one four-week rotation; the international fee is $4,000 per four-week elective plus a $150 application processing fee. Participating departments include Pediatrics, Orthopaedics, Emergency Medicine, IM, and multiple surgical and medical subspecialties. Credit-bearing, integrates students into clinical teams at University of Colorado Hospital and Children's Hospital Colorado.

Each visiting student is limited to a single four-week rotation across the academic year, and participating departments include Emergency Medicine, Pediatrics (Doernbecher Children's Hospital), Surgery, Radiation Oncology, Neurosurgery, and Internal Medicine. title: University of Florida College of Medicine International Visiting Student
specialty: Multi-specialty (2- or 4-week electives) at Gainesville and Jacksonville
shortDescription: UF's International Visiting Student Program admits final-year non-US med students to 2- or 4-week rotations. USMLE Step 1 firm requirement.
fullDescription: The University of Florida College of Medicine runs a dedicated International Visiting Student Program open to final-year medical students enrolled at non-US medical schools, distinct from its US VSLO pathway. Applicants must have completed the six core clerkships (Family Medicine, IM, OB/GYN, Peds, Psychiatry, Surgery) at their home institution before the start of the UF rotation. A USMLE Step 1 passing score or 'PASS' result is mandatory. The home institution must have an executed affiliation agreement with UF before any rotation request can be processed, and this can take months to arrange. Rotations are offered in two-week or four-week blocks at the Gainesville and Jacksonville campuses through their respective departments. UF does not provide housing. The international track is a structured credit-bearing clinical elective, not a shadowing or observation program, and emphasizes full clinical-team integration with appropriate direct patient-care involvement under faculty supervision.

title: Cedars-Sinai Pre-Med Volunteer Shadowing Program
shortDescription: Structured ten-week shadowing for pre-med undergraduates at Cedars-Sinai, gated by 100 prior volunteer hours.
fullDescription: Cedars-Sinai Medical Center operates a formal Pre-Med Volunteer Shadowing Program for undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students planning to attend medical school. Applicants must first be existing Cedars-Sinai volunteers and complete at least 100 hours of hospital volunteer service before becoming eligible to apply. Once accepted, students join a ten-week cohort of roughly thirty peers who are paired with physician mentors in clinics and operating rooms to observe clinical practice. Cohorts meet weekly for facilitated peer discussion and Q&A sessions with physicians. The program is led by Jason Cohen, MD, and is one of the longer-running formally structured pre-med shadowing pathways at a large US academic medical center. Pre-med applicants seeking clinical exposure, a hospital reference, and a structured peer group in preparation for medical school often cite this program as a West Coast flagship.
duration: 10 weeks, after 100 hours of prior Cedars-Sinai volunteer service
cost: No program fee; unpaid volunteer role

page_excerpts.text:
Pre-Med Volunteer Shadowing Program — Empowering Healthcare Mentorship
The Pre-Med Student Volunteer Shadowing Program, under the leadership of Jason Cohen, MD, is for undergraduate and post-baccalaureate students who are planning to attend medical school. The program provides shadowing and mentorship, as well as a glimpse into what it means to be a physician today.
What to Expect
Over the 8-10 week program, volunteers who are pre-med students will be assigned to a team of mentors within a specific specialty. Volunteers may shadow physicians on patient clinic visits, rounds, grand rounds, collaboration meetings and even in the operating room if they are assigned to a surgical specialty.

title: University Hospitals Cleveland Visiting Medical Student Program
shortDescription: University Hospitals Cleveland Undergraduate Medical Education office with Visiting Medical Students and David Satcher Clerkship sub-pages.
fullDescription: University Hospitals Cleveland Medical Center hosts visiting medical students across its flagship campus and three specialty hospitals: UH Rainbow Babies & Children's Hospital, UH Seidman Cancer Center, and UH MacDonald Women's Hospital. The program accepts domestic medical students who have completed their core clinical training and will be in the fourth year of medical education at their LCME- or AOA-accredited medical school. Applications must be submitted through AAMC VSLO with 'University Hospitals Cleveland Medical Center' as host; Case Western Reserve students register through the CWRU Registrar instead. On-service responsibilities include patient histories and physicals, procedures with supervision, didactics, and department conferences. UHCMC also runs the David Satcher Clerkship for 4-8 US-citizen students from July to October. CWRU School of Medicine does not directly accept visiting students; UHCMC, Cleveland Clinic, VA Northeast Ohio, and MetroHealth collectively handle all Cleveland-area visiting applications.
eligibilitySummary: UH Cleveland Undergraduate Medical Education Office hosts visiting medical students from accredited US institutions. Includes the David Satcher Clerkship sub-program for URM students. See sub-nav pages for specific eligibility requirements.
stepRequirements: Step 1 or COMLEX Level 1 required by most departments
